Module 3:
Quick Launch, Part 2
!
Executive Summary
After learning the basics of the Quick Launch, I’m now going to walk you
through three different examples.
Example 1: Jeff’s “Tax Sale” - this classic example can be used around
any event in your life. Another PLF owner used a story about a pipe
bursting in his home.
• 	Timing
• 	Super quick with very little effort
• 	Two days - four emails and a landing page
• 	Offer - discount off normal price
• 	Reason why
• 	Story
• 	Scarcity
• 	Advanced: “Future Pacing” - a copy technique
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 3 --

I had an internal memo to my partner explaining the tax bill surprise. I
mentioned that we should use this as an opportunity to offer a sale and tell
them exactly why.
• 	Email 1 - April 14th: went out 36-48 hours after I came up with the idea
• 	Copy in the email should be very conversational
• 	Triggers: story based, reason why, scarcity
• 	Send the prospect straight to the order page right off a “reason why”
email
• 	At the end I tied it back to the topic of the market we were selling into
• 	Email 2 - April 15th
• 	Hitting them with more scarcity, at this point there was only one day
left
• 	Email 3 - April 16th
• 	I basically did the same thing again
• 	“You should have jumped on board because you would have made
some money already”
• 	Big time scarcity, only 8 hours left - sent them directly to landing
page
• 	Email 4 - April 16th (later in the day)
!
2	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 3 --

• 	Although this is the 4th email in the series it’s only the 2nd dedicated
email
• 	Here I used the “reluctant hero” strategy by saying I’m sending this
final email reminder so I don’t get the angry responses by people
who missed the sale
• 	“Future pacing”: get people to walk forward in time and imagine the
negative place they would be if they didn’t purchase from us -
missing out on the opportunity
• 	Sales page - plain and simple
• 	Repeat the story
• 	Repeat the offer
• 	Repeat scarcity
• 	Show testimonials
In the end, any unexpected thing that went wrong is a good story - however
you never want to be begging. Use humor and it can be very effective, all
without hurting perceived value. Enjoy!
!
3	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.

-- 3 of 3 --
